idea.title: Cargo Chain Junction
idea.hook: Each turn flip ONE rail switch to route incoming cargo wagons into the matching colored depot — wrong depot blows the wagon and one of your three sidings.
idea.hook_ru: Каждый ход переключи ОДНУ стрелку, чтобы направить вагоны в депо нужного цвета — ошибка взрывает вагон и уничтожает один из трёх запасных путей.
idea.prototype: Top-down rail board 8 columns x 10 rows with 5 colored depots arranged along the south + east edges. Each turn 1-3 wagons spawn in fixed lanes from the north and advance 1 tile south per END TURN press. 9 junctions on rows 2/4/6 route wagons left or right when crossed. Player flips one junction per turn (tap). Correct depot = +10 + combo bonus; wrong depot = wagon explodes and one of 3 sidings destroyed. Difficulty escalates: spawn count rises by 1 every 8 turns, capped at 3 per turn. Daily seed drives wagon color sequence so all players face the same cargo on a given day.
idea.why_novel: First fully turn-based router in the gallery — every shipped routing/sorting game on Tims Arcade has been real-time (Token Triage, Junk Food Poker, etc.). The one-flip-per-turn budget makes each click a tiny gamble; the rest of the board state is fixed until the player commits END TURN. No clock anywhere — pressure comes from spawn-count escalation and the 3-life budget. Daily seed gives a friend-share angle ('I got 240, you got 180 on the same wagons').
idea.non_timer_pressure: Lives (sidings, 3 -> 0 = GAMEOVER) + per-turn flip budget (exactly 1 flip allowed per END TURN). Spawn rate escalates every 8 turns. Zero countdown timer anywhere.

sources.title: Cargo Chain Junction
sources.snippet: Entry #2 — Top-down rail board ~8 tracks wide with 5 colored depots along edges. Each turn 1-3 new wagons enter from the north spawn moving south at 1 tile/turn. Player gets ONE switch flip per turn. Correct depot = score + cargo cleared; wrong depot = depot rejects + wagon explodes + lose 1 of 3 sidings.

sources.title: Rail/logistics sims are a useful simplification target
sources.snippet: Full railway sims are too heavy, but route optimization and cargo/passenger chains can be compressed into a portal-friendly loop. Prototype a tiny route-drawing economy: connect nodes, earn from cargo, upgrade stations, avoid congestion.

sources.title: Бюро Сортировки — sorting as a Steam-page mechanic
sources.snippet: Demo of Бюро Сортировки published — sorting/routing mechanics get organic traction with no paid acquisition. Confirms casual-routing is a winning genre frame.

synthesizer_brief: Turn-based rail-switch puzzle. 8-column board, 5 colored depots, 9 junctions on alternating rows. Each turn the player flips ONE junction then presses END TURN, which advances all wagons 1 tile and spawns 1-3 new colored wagons from north. Matching depot = score+combo. Wrong depot = wagon explodes, sidings--. 3 sidings lost = GAMEOVER. Endless score-attack, best persists. Difficulty rises via spawn-count escalation (every 8 turns), no timer anywhere. Daily seed = identical wagon-color sequence across players on the same date. Mutates the rail-sorter genre by stripping out the timer and making one click matter.

alternatives.title: Ice Fracture Foreman
alternatives.hook: Tap to crack glacier panels along stress lines before the cargo barge below crushes against a fresh shelf.
alternatives.why_novel: Procedural ice-fracture toy with shrink-target pressure — strong screen-space VFX without expensive simulation

alternatives.title: Tiny Model Dodger
alternatives.hook: You're a tiny local LLM weaving through a swarm of giant frontier-model orbs that fire token-cost beams.
alternatives.why_novel: Bullet-hell themed on local-vs-frontier AI cost — agent-economics is the day's strongest cultural signal

alternatives.title: Hook Test Lab
alternatives.hook: Stitch a 30-second player-hook from cards and watch a simulated tester decide if they keep playing.
alternatives.why_novel: Self-referential card-drafting where the deliverable is a hook reel; tester-reaction loop instead of pure stat block

alternatives.title: Shader Polish Sprint
alternatives.hook: Drag glow/blur/parallax sliders to make a stale prototype 'feel like a real game' within the polish budget.
alternatives.why_novel: Toy mimicking the Subnautica-2 lesson: cheap VFX sells a simple mechanic. Players see live before/after the slider change.
